page: Amazon Web Services c7g.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by 64 physical cores of the custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton3 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features a dedicated CPU allocation with one thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c7g.metal demonstrates strong multi-core performance. Multi-core CPU tests via stress-ng and Geekbench place the server in the strong and top-tier categories respectively, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 21025.0 and a software compilation score of 60006.0. Memory performance is also a key strength, with DDR5 memory bandwidth reaching a top-tier PassMark memory score and read-only small block cached bandwidth of 6205.93 GB/sec. Single-core performance, such as Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core, remains in the average tier.

The server is configured for workloads requiring high multi-threaded compute density and memory bandwidth without the need for local storage or GPU acceleration. It is suited for software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and static web serving, where it achieved a throughput of 76.33 GB/sec. However, text generation for small LLM models is a notable bottleneck, registering in the poor tier at 35.44 tokens/sec. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture delivers high physical core density and memory throughput relative to its resource profile, though workloads requiring local NVMe storage or x86-specific instruction sets will require alternative configurations.
description: Amazon Web Services c7g.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton3 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. The server does not include local storage or GPU hardware. In benchmark testing, the c7g.metal delivers top-tier multi-core performance, notably in software compilation and memory bandwidth, alongside strong database and static web serving throughput. Single-core operations and small-model LLM text generation represent comparative performance tradeoffs. This bare-metal instance is designed for highly parallelized workloads, multi-threaded decompression, software building, and memory-bound applications that do not require local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.metal is a bare-metal server with 64 ARMv8 Graviton3 cores, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 64 ARMv8 Graviton3 cores, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A bare-metal ARMv8 server featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and high-bandwidth DDR5 memory for parallelized compute workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARMv8 AWS Graviton3 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz
128 GB of high-bandwidth DDR5 memory with 2.0 GB per core
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Top-tier multi-core performance in software compilation and memory bandwidth
Strong performance in database operations and static web serving
Average single-core performance with poor small-model LLM text generation speeds
